title: trifolium vestitum
intro: Trifolium vestitum, also known as the hairy clover, is a perennial legume native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its hairy leaves and purple flowers. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Trifolium vestitum include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues, making it a valuable herb for promoting overall well-being. Trifolium vestitum can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
